items.query: What is the maximum single-invoice spend a Cost Centre Manager can approve?

gt_supporting_facts.text: Cost Centre Managers may approve a single invoice up to USD 25,000.

items.query: Which role signs off on capital expenditure requests above USD 1 million?
items.gt_answer: The CFO

gt_supporting_facts.text: CAPEX requests above USD 1,000,000 require CFO approval.

items.query: For a USD 400,000 vendor contract, which two approvers are required per the Delegation of Authority?
items.gt_answer: The relevant Business Unit VP and the Group Financial Controller

gt_supporting_facts.text: Vendor contracts between USD 250,000 and USD 500,000 require BU VP approval.
Vendor contracts above USD 250,000 also require Group Financial Controller approval.

items.query: Does the Delegation of Authority Matrix apply to all ACME subsidiaries?
items.gt_answer: Yes, it applies to all consolidated subsidiaries

gt_supporting_facts.text: The Delegation of Authority Matrix applies to all consolidated ACME subsidiaries worldwide.

items.query: Which document version of the Delegation of Authority Matrix is currently in force?
items.gt_answer: Version 4

gt_supporting_facts.text: The Delegation of Authority Matrix in force is version 4, effective January 2025.

items.query: What is the sourcing threshold above which a formal tender is required?

gt_supporting_facts.text: Any sourcing event above USD 100,000 requires a formal competitive tender.

items.query: How many suppliers must be invited to a competitive tender for goods valued between USD 100k and USD 1M?
items.gt_answer: At least three suppliers

gt_supporting_facts.text: Tenders between USD 100,000 and USD 1,000,000 require a minimum of three invited suppliers.

items.query: When is a single-source procurement justification memo required?
items.gt_answer: For any award without a competitive tender above USD 50,000

gt_supporting_facts.text: A single-source justification memo is required for any award above USD 50,000 without competitive tender.

items.query: What is the minimum retention period for procurement bid records?
items.gt_answer: Seven years

gt_supporting_facts.text: Procurement bid records must be retained for a minimum of seven years.
Records Retention Schedule mandates seven-year retention for sourcing files.

items.query: Who owns the Procurement Policy and is responsible for its annual review?
items.gt_answer: The Chief Procurement Officer

gt_supporting_facts.text: The Chief Procurement Officer owns the Procurement Policy and reviews it annually.

items.query: Compare the tender requirements for IT services versus professional services.
items.gt_answer: IT services above USD 100k require competitive tender with security assessment; professional services above USD 100k require tender but no security assessment

gt_supporting_facts.text: IT services above USD 100,000 require a competitive tender with an information-security assessment.
Professional services above USD 100,000 require a competitive tender but not an information-security assessment.

items.query: Is emergency procurement permitted?
items.gt_answer: Yes, with retroactive approval within five business days by the Chief Procurement Officer

gt_supporting_facts.text: Emergency procurement is permitted when a business-continuity threat exists.
Emergency procurement requires retroactive approval by the CPO within five business days.

items.query: What KYC documents must a new vendor provide during onboarding?
items.gt_answer: Certificate of incorporation, ultimate beneficial ownership disclosure, and tax registration

gt_supporting_facts.text: Vendors must provide a certificate of incorporation at onboarding.
Vendors must disclose ultimate beneficial ownership at onboarding.
Vendors must provide a tax registration certificate at onboarding.

items.query: Against which sanctions lists are new vendors screened?
items.gt_answer: OFAC, EU consolidated list, and UN Security Council list

gt_supporting_facts.text: New vendors are screened against the OFAC SDN list.
New vendors are screened against the EU consolidated sanctions list.
New vendors are screened against the UN Security Council sanctions list.

items.query: Which vendor tier requires an on-site audit?
items.gt_answer: Tier 1 (critical) vendors

gt_supporting_facts.text: Tier 1 (critical) vendors require an on-site audit as part of due diligence.

items.query: How often must Tier 2 vendors be re-assessed?
items.gt_answer: Every two years

gt_supporting_facts.text: Tier 2 vendors must be re-assessed at least every two years.

items.query: What is the required action when a sanctions screening hit is confirmed?
items.gt_answer: Freeze payments, escalate to the Chief Compliance Officer, and file a report with the relevant financial-intelligence unit if required

gt_supporting_facts.text: Confirmed sanctions hits require immediate freeze of all payments to the vendor.
Confirmed sanctions hits must be escalated to the Chief Compliance Officer.
Confirmed sanctions hits may require reporting to the relevant financial-intelligence unit.

items.query: Are politically exposed persons (PEPs) prohibited as vendor beneficial owners?
items.gt_answer: No, but they trigger enhanced due diligence and Chief Compliance Officer sign-off

gt_supporting_facts.text: PEP-linked vendors are not automatically prohibited.
PEP-linked vendors require enhanced due diligence.
PEP-linked vendor engagements require sign-off from the Chief Compliance Officer.

items.query: What is the maximum vendor risk score that still allows automated approval?
items.gt_answer: Below 40 out of 100

gt_supporting_facts.text: Vendor risk scores below 40 out of 100 are eligible for automated onboarding approval.

items.query: Compare the exit-plan requirements for Tier 1 and Tier 3 vendors.
items.gt_answer: Tier 1 vendors require a documented and tested exit plan; Tier 3 vendors require only a documented exit plan

gt_supporting_facts.text: Tier 1 vendors require a documented and annually tested exit plan.
Tier 3 vendors require a documented exit plan but no scheduled testing.

items.query: What is the per-diem meal allowance for domestic travel in the United States?
items.gt_answer: USD 75 per day

gt_supporting_facts.text: The per-diem meal allowance for US domestic travel is USD 75 per day.

items.query: What is the per-diem meal allowance for international travel to London?
items.gt_answer: GBP 65 per day

gt_supporting_facts.text: The per-diem meal allowance for travel to London is GBP 65 per day.

items.query: Are alcohol expenses reimbursable?
items.gt_answer: Only when incurred as part of an approved client entertainment event

gt_supporting_facts.text: Alcohol is reimbursable only within an approved client entertainment expense.

items.query: By what deadline must expense reports be submitted after a business trip?
items.gt_answer: Within 30 calendar days of trip end

gt_supporting_facts.text: Expense reports must be submitted within 30 calendar days of the trip end date.

items.query: Which class of flight is permitted for a Director travelling more than eight hours?
items.gt_answer: Business class

gt_supporting_facts.text: Directors may travel business class on flights exceeding eight hours.

items.query: May employees book flights outside the corporate travel agency?
items.gt_answer: Only with pre-approval by the manager and Travel Manager

gt_supporting_facts.text: Bookings outside the corporate travel agency require prior manager approval.
Bookings outside the corporate travel agency also require Travel Manager approval.

items.query: What is the receipt threshold above which employees must attach a scanned receipt?
items.gt_answer: USD 25

gt_supporting_facts.text: Scanned receipts must be attached for any expense above USD 25.

items.query: How does travel-policy compliance differ between full-time employees and contractors?
items.gt_answer: Contractors follow their master services agreement; employees follow ACME Travel Policy

gt_supporting_facts.text: Full-time employees are subject to the ACME Travel Policy.
Contractor travel is governed by the terms of their master services agreement, not ACME Travel Policy.

items.query: How many key SOX controls does ACME maintain across the revenue cycle?
items.gt_answer: 24 key controls

gt_supporting_facts.text: ACME maintains 24 key SOX controls across the revenue cycle.

items.query: How frequently is control R-07 (three-way match) tested?
items.gt_answer: Quarterly

gt_supporting_facts.text: Control R-07 (three-way match) is tested on a quarterly cadence.

items.query: Under ASC 606, how is revenue recognized for a software subscription?
items.gt_answer: Rateably over the subscription term

gt_supporting_facts.text: Under ASC 606, revenue for software subscriptions is recognised rateably over the subscription term.

items.query: For an implementation service bundled with a software subscription, when is that service revenue recognized?
items.gt_answer: When the implementation is delivered, if it is a distinct performance obligation

gt_supporting_facts.text: Implementation services bundled with a subscription are distinct if they are not required to use the software.
Distinct implementation services are recognised when delivered.

items.query: By which working day must all sub-ledger journals be posted for the monthly close?
items.gt_answer: WD-2

gt_supporting_facts.text: All sub-ledger journals must be posted by working day WD-2.

items.query: Which team owns the monthly close calendar?
items.gt_answer: The Global Controllership team

gt_supporting_facts.text: The Global Controllership team owns the monthly close calendar.

items.query: What is the deadline for management reporting after quarter end?
items.gt_answer: WD-5 for internal reporting, WD-8 for consolidated group reporting

gt_supporting_facts.text: Internal quarterly management reporting is due on WD-5 after quarter end.
Consolidated group quarterly reporting is due on WD-8 after quarter end.

items.query: Are journal entries above USD 500,000 subject to additional review?
items.gt_answer: Yes, they require dual review by the Controller and a delegated approver

gt_supporting_facts.text: Journal entries above USD 500,000 require dual review.
Dual review is performed by the Controller and a delegated approver.

items.query: Which GL account is used to record software subscription revenue?
items.gt_answer: GL 4110 — Subscription Revenue

gt_supporting_facts.text: GL account 4110 is designated Subscription Revenue.

items.query: Which cost centre owns the finance transformation programme?
items.gt_answer: Cost Centre CC-72110

gt_supporting_facts.text: Cost centre CC-72110 owns the Finance Transformation programme.

items.query: What is the CAPEX capitalization threshold for IT hardware?
items.gt_answer: USD 5,000 per item

gt_supporting_facts.text: The CAPEX capitalisation threshold for IT hardware is USD 5,000 per item.

items.query: What is the CAPEX capitalization threshold for internal-use software development?
items.gt_answer: USD 50,000 per project

gt_supporting_facts.text: Internal-use software development capitalisation begins at USD 50,000 per project.

items.query: Compare the depreciation approach for IT hardware versus office furniture.
items.gt_answer: IT hardware is depreciated over 3 years straight-line; office furniture is depreciated over 7 years straight-line

gt_supporting_facts.text: IT hardware is depreciated over 3 years on a straight-line basis.
Office furniture is depreciated over 7 years on a straight-line basis.

items.query: By when must annual budget submissions be complete?
items.gt_answer: By November 15

gt_supporting_facts.text: Annual budget submissions must be complete by November 15.

items.query: How many forecast reforecasts are performed per year?
items.gt_answer: Three: after Q1, Q2, and Q3

gt_supporting_facts.text: ACME runs three reforecasts per year: after Q1, Q2 and Q3.

items.query: What variance threshold triggers a mandatory variance explanation to the CFO office?
items.gt_answer: 10% or USD 500,000 whichever is lower

gt_supporting_facts.text: A budget variance above 10% requires a written explanation to the CFO office.
A budget variance above USD 500,000 requires a written explanation to the CFO office.

items.query: What is the maximum value of a gift an employee may accept without pre-approval?
items.gt_answer: USD 100

gt_supporting_facts.text: Employees may accept gifts up to USD 100 in value without pre-approval.

items.query: Must gifts to government officials always be pre-approved?
items.gt_answer: Yes, regardless of value

gt_supporting_facts.text: Any gift to a government official must be pre-approved regardless of value.

items.query: Which register is used to record gifts and hospitality above USD 100?
items.gt_answer: The Gifts and Hospitality Register

gt_supporting_facts.text: Gifts and hospitality above USD 100 must be logged in the Gifts and Hospitality Register.

items.query: How does the FCPA differ from the UK Bribery Act in scope of coverage?
items.gt_answer: The UK Bribery Act criminalises private-sector bribery as well as public-sector; the FCPA covers primarily foreign public officials

gt_supporting_facts.text: The FCPA primarily addresses bribery of foreign public officials.
The UK Bribery Act covers both public and private-sector bribery.

items.query: How can employees report a suspected code-of-conduct violation anonymously?
items.gt_answer: Through the Speak-Up helpline operated by an independent third party

gt_supporting_facts.text: The Speak-Up helpline allows anonymous reporting of suspected violations.
The Speak-Up helpline is operated by an independent third-party provider.

items.query: Are retaliation actions against whistleblowers ever permitted?
items.gt_answer: No, retaliation is strictly prohibited and grounds for termination

gt_supporting_facts.text: Retaliation against whistleblowers is strictly prohibited and grounds for termination.

items.query: What is ACME's arm's-length pricing method for intercompany services?
items.gt_answer: The cost-plus method with a 5% markup

gt_supporting_facts.text: Intercompany services are priced using the cost-plus method with a 5% markup.

items.query: For an EU B2B service invoice cross-border, who accounts for VAT?
items.gt_answer: The customer, under the reverse charge mechanism

gt_supporting_facts.text: EU cross-border B2B service invoices apply the reverse charge mechanism.
Under the reverse charge, the customer accounts for VAT.

items.query: What is the standard VAT rate applied for supplies within France?

gt_supporting_facts.text: The standard French VAT rate is 20%.

items.query: Which document records the transfer-pricing benchmarks used each year?
items.gt_answer: The master file and local file transfer-pricing documentation

Local file documentation captures country-specific transfer-pricing benchmarks.

items.query: When must the local file transfer-pricing documentation be finalized?

gt_supporting_facts.text: Local file transfer-pricing documentation must be finalised by each jurisdiction's corporate tax return deadline.

items.query: What is the maximum counterparty exposure allowed to a single non-investment-grade bank?

gt_supporting_facts.text: Maximum counterparty exposure to a single non-investment-grade bank is USD 25 million.

items.query: Which currencies is ACME's FX hedging programme authorized to hedge?
items.gt_answer: USD, EUR, GBP, JPY, and CNH

gt_supporting_facts.text: ACME's FX hedging programme is authorised for USD, EUR, GBP, JPY and CNH.

items.query: What is the standard customer payment term for enterprise contracts?

gt_supporting_facts.text: The standard customer payment term for enterprise contracts is Net 45.

items.query: At what days-past-due does an AR balance become subject to Collections escalation?

gt_supporting_facts.text: AR balances 60 days past due are escalated to Collections.

AR write-off additionally requires Collections activity exhaustion and Controller approval.

items.query: How is 3-way match performed in Accounts Payable?
items.gt_answer: By matching the purchase order, goods receipt, and vendor invoice

gt_supporting_facts.text: 3-way match compares the purchase order, goods receipt, and vendor invoice.

items.query: What is the tolerance for price variance between PO and invoice in AP?
items.gt_answer: 3% or USD 100 whichever is greater

AP price variance is tolerated up to USD 100 between PO and invoice.

items.query: What is the required customer credit limit review cadence?
items.gt_answer: Every 12 months, or immediately upon a credit downgrade

Customer credit limits are re-reviewed immediately upon a credit downgrade.

items.query: How long must invoices be retained under the records retention schedule?

items.query: How long must general ledger data be retained?

items.query: Compare the retention periods for HR employment records versus payroll records.

Payroll records must be retained for ten years.

items.query: Who does Internal Audit functionally report to?

gt_supporting_facts.text: Internal Audit reports functionally to the Audit Committee of the Board.

items.query: What is the minimum frequency of Internal Audit engagements for material subsidiaries?
items.gt_answer: At least once every three years

gt_supporting_facts.text: Material subsidiaries are audited at least once every three years.

items.query: What is ACME's enterprise risk appetite for reputational risk?
items.gt_answer: Low — ACME accepts minimal reputational exposure

gt_supporting_facts.text: ACME's risk appetite for reputational risk is stated as low.

items.query: Which KRI monitors third-party concentration risk?

KRI-14 is defined as the percentage of spend concentrated in the top-five vendors.

items.query: What is the KRI-14 amber threshold?

gt_supporting_facts.text: KRI-14 breaches amber at 40% spend concentration in top-five vendors.

items.query: What is the KRI-14 red threshold?

items.query: What is the retention period for job-applicant data of unsuccessful candidates?
items.gt_answer: 12 months, unless the candidate consents to longer retention

Retention beyond 12 months requires the candidate's explicit consent.

items.query: Who is ACME's Data Protection Officer?
items.gt_answer: The Group Data Protection Officer based in Ireland

gt_supporting_facts.text: The Group Data Protection Officer is based in Ireland.

items.query: What is the maximum response time to a subject-access request under GDPR?
items.gt_answer: One month from receipt, extendable by two further months for complex requests

gt_supporting_facts.text: GDPR subject access requests must be answered within one month of receipt.
Complex GDPR subject access requests can be extended by up to two additional months.

items.query: When does a vendor engagement require both a competitive tender AND a Tier 1 risk assessment?
items.gt_answer: When the contract value exceeds USD 100,000 and the vendor supplies a critical service

gt_supporting_facts.text: Contracts above USD 100,000 require a competitive tender.
Tier 1 (critical) vendor status requires a Tier 1 risk assessment.

CAPEX Policy governs justification and business-case documentation.
Tier 1 vendor engagements require Third-Party Risk Framework due diligence.

Invoices, including VAT invoices, must be retained for ten years.
